description: Headless macOS VM on Linux (KVM) for Flutter iOS iteration. Tiered CI: Tier 1 = dart analyze + flutter test on Linux host (instant); Tier 2 = flutter build ios in VM (~30s); Tier 3 = flutter run on USB-connected iPhone via VM passthrough.

vm_allocation.rationale: Leaves 6 cores + 7GB for Linux host during parallel operation

vm.rationale: Ventura is most stable in OSX-KVM; Sonoma works but has more driver friction

milestones.title: VM Bootstrap
milestones.description: Clone OSX-KVM, create disk image, install macOS Ventura, configure SSH for headless access.

model.rationale: Step-by-step shell scripting with clear verification

tasks.title: Clone OSX-KVM and fetch Ventura recovery image
acceptance_criteria: OSX-KVM repo cloned to $HOME/OSX-KVM
BaseSystem.dmg downloaded via fetch-macOS-v2.py (Ventura)
BaseSystem.img created via dmg2img
mac_hdd_ng.img (120G qcow2) created at $HOME/OSX-KVM/mac_hdd_ng.img

tasks.title: Write QEMU launch script (headless + VNC for setup)
acceptance_criteria: scripts/infra/launch-vm.sh exists and is executable
Flags: -enable-kvm, -m 8192, -smp 6, -machine q35, -cpu Penryn
Network: -netdev user,id=net0,hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22 + virtio-net-pci
Storage: OpenCore.qcow2 + mac_hdd_ng.img on virtio
Default mode: -display none -daemonize (headless); --vnc flag adds -display vnc=:1 for setup
PID file written to /tmp/osx-kvm.pid

tasks.title: Document first-boot VNC setup procedure
acceptance_criteria: FIRST-BOOT.md documents: launch with --vnc, connect via VNC on :5901
Documents: macOS install to disk, create user account, enable Remote Login (SSH)
Documents: sudo systemsetup -setcomputersleep Never (disable sleep)
Documents: baseline snapshot command after setup complete

tasks.title: Configure SSH alias and keypair for passwordless access
tasks.verification_command: ssh mac-vm 'uname -s' 2>/dev/null | grep -q Darwin && echo PASS
acceptance_criteria: ~/.ssh/config contains Host mac-vm entry: HostName localhost, Port 2222, IdentityFile ~/.ssh/mac_vm_key
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 creates ~/.ssh/mac_vm_key
Public key copied to VM authorized_keys via ssh-copy-id
ssh mac-vm 'uname -s' returns Darwin without password prompt

tasks.title: Create baseline VM snapshot
acceptance_criteria: scripts/infra/snapshot.sh create|restore|list subcommands work
Snapshot named 'baseline' exists in mac_hdd_ng.img after clean macOS install
Snapshot named 'toolchain' created after I1.2 completes

milestones.title: Flutter iOS Toolchain in VM
milestones.description: Install Xcode CLI tools, Flutter SDK (matching pubspec), CocoaPods, and Fastlane inside the macOS VM. Verify with flutter doctor.

model.rationale: macOS toolchain installation with version pinning

tasks.title: Write VM toolchain setup script

model.rationale: Script runs over SSH in VM

acceptance_criteria: scripts/infra/setup-toolchain.sh runs via: ssh mac-vm 'bash -s' < scripts/infra/setup-toolchain.sh
Installs Xcode Command Line Tools: xcode-select --install
Accepts Xcode license: sudo xcodebuild -license accept
Installs Homebrew if absent
Installs rbenv + Ruby 3.x for CocoaPods/Fastlane gem isolation
gem install cocoapods fastlane
ssh mac-vm 'xcodebuild -version' returns Xcode version string

features.title: Flutter SDK pinned to project version

tasks.title: Install Flutter SDK in VM matching pubspec constraint (^3.11.0)
acceptance_criteria: Flutter SDK cloned to ~/flutter in VM, stable channel
flutter --version reports 3.x.x matching ^3.11.0
PATH updated in VM ~/.zshrc / ~/.bashrc
ssh mac-vm 'flutter doctor' shows iOS toolchain green (no Xcode issues)
ssh mac-vm 'flutter doctor' shows Android toolchain skipped (not needed)

tasks.title: Verify project builds in VM (no-codesign)
tasks.verification_command: ssh mac-vm 'cd ~/runthru && flutter build ios --no-codesign 2>&1 | tail -5'
acceptance_criteria: Project synced to ~/runthru in VM (see I1.3)
flutter build ios --no-codesign exits 0
build/ios/iphoneos/Runner.app exists in VM
dart analyze lib/ reports zero issues from VM
tasks.notes: Depends on I1.3 for code sync; run this after sync is working

milestones.title: Code Sync Pipeline
milestones.description: Incremental rsync from Linux host to VM on file change. Watch daemon uses inotifywait. Tier 1 (analyze+test) runs on host; Tier 2 (build) runs in VM after sync.

tasks.title: Write scripts/infra/sync.sh
tasks.verification_command: bash scripts/infra/sync.sh && ssh mac-vm 'test -f ~/runthru/pubspec.yaml' && echo PASS
acceptance_criteria: Excludes: .dart_tool/, build/, .git/, .fvm/, ios/Pods/, android/.gradle/
Uses rsync -avz --delete for incremental transfer
Target: mac-vm:~/runthru/
Exits non-zero if SSH connection fails
Prints bytes transferred and elapsed time

tasks.title: Write scripts/infra/watch.sh — inotifywait loop with tiered actions
acceptance_criteria: Watches lib/, test/, pubspec.yaml for modify/create/delete events
Debounce: 1s settle window before triggering (avoids multi-event storms on save)
Tier 1 (always): dart analyze --fatal-infos on Linux host; prints PASS/FAIL
Tier 1 (always): flutter test on Linux host if no analyze errors
Tier 2 (on --build flag or manual key press 'b'): sync + ssh mac-vm flutter build ios --no-codesign
Ctrl-C cleanly kills background jobs
Requires: inotify-tools package (prints install hint if absent)

milestones.title: USB iPhone Passthrough
milestones.description: Pass through USB-connected iPhone from Linux host to macOS VM so flutter run and Xcode can deploy directly to device.

model.rationale: udev + QEMU USB config requires precision

epics.title: udev rules + QEMU USB host device

tasks.title: Write udev rule granting QEMU access to Apple USB devices
acceptance_criteria: Rule: SUBSYSTEM=="usb", ATTR{idVendor}=="05ac", GROUP="kvm", MODE="0664"
udevadm control --reload-rules applied
setup-usb-passthrough.sh idempotent (re-running does not duplicate the rule)

features.title: QEMU USB passthrough in launch script

tasks.title: Add --usb-passthrough flag to launch-vm.sh
tasks.verification_command: ssh mac-vm 'system_profiler SPUSBDataType 2>/dev/null | grep -i iphone' | grep -qi iphone && echo PASS
acceptance_criteria: launch-vm.sh --usb-passthrough detects connected iPhone via lsusb (vendor 05ac)
Passes -device usb-host,vendorid=0x05ac,productid=<detected> to QEMU
If no iPhone connected: prints warning, starts VM without passthrough
VM must be restarted to pick up newly connected iPhone (documented in FIRST-BOOT.md)
ssh mac-vm 'system_profiler SPUSBDataType' shows iPhone when connected and VM started with --usb-passthrough

milestones.title: CI Loop Scripts
milestones.description: Tier 2 (build) and Tier 3 (device run) scripts. Unified ci.sh entry point with --tier flag. Output is human-readable and machine-parseable (exit codes).

tasks.title: Write scripts/infra/build-ios.sh
acceptance_criteria: Calls sync.sh first, then ssh mac-vm 'cd ~/runthru && flutter build ios --no-codesign'
Streams build output to stdout in real time (no buffering)
Exits with VM flutter build exit code
Prints elapsed time on completion
Prints clear PASS / FAIL banner at end

tasks.title: Write scripts/infra/run-device.sh
acceptance_criteria: Detects connected iPhone device ID via: ssh mac-vm 'flutter devices' | grep ios
If no device found: prints actionable error (check USB passthrough, trust prompt)
Runs: ssh -t mac-vm 'cd ~/runthru && flutter run -d <device_id>'
-t flag allocates pseudo-TTY so hot reload (r) and quit (q) work interactively
Ctrl-C on Linux host propagates SIGINT to flutter run in VM

tasks.title: Write scripts/infra/ci.sh — unified entry point
acceptance_criteria: ci.sh --tier 1: dart analyze + flutter test on host
ci.sh --tier 2: sync + iOS build in VM
ci.sh --tier 3: sync + flutter run on device
ci.sh --watch: starts watch.sh daemon
ci.sh --status: prints VM up/down, iPhone connected yes/no, last build result
--help prints usage

milestones.title: Headless Hardening
milestones.description: systemd unit for VM auto-start, resource tuning, snapshot workflow, and optional SSH MCP server integration for AI-native CI.

tasks.title: Write ~/.config/systemd/user/osx-kvm.service
acceptance_criteria: Type=forking; PIDFile=/tmp/osx-kvm.pid
ExecStart runs launch-vm.sh (headless, no --usb-passthrough)
ExecStop sends ACPI shutdown: ssh mac-vm 'sudo shutdown -h now'
Restart=on-failure; RestartSec=10
scripts/infra/install-service.sh installs + enables the unit
systemctl --user start/stop/status osx-kvm works

tasks.title: Add memory ballooning and CPU tuning to launch-vm.sh
acceptance_criteria: -device virtio-balloon-pci for dynamic memory (macOS can return unused RAM)
-cpu host,kvm=on if host CPU is Intel (better perf than Penryn emulation)
Fallback: -cpu Penryn if host is not Intel or host mode causes boot failure
QEMU memory backend uses -mem-prealloc for lower latency on 8GB allocation
Document in launch-vm.sh header: expected idle RAM usage on host ~6GB when VM active

features.title: Optional: SSH MCP server for AI-native CI

tasks.title: Document SSH MCP server setup for Claude Code direct VM access
acceptance_criteria: Documents mcp-server-ssh or equivalent (e.g. mcp-server-shell with SSH wrapper)
Shows .claude/settings.json mcpServers entry for mac-vm SSH access
Explains: Claude Code can then run flutter build / dart analyze in VM directly
Security note: restrict MCP server to mac-vm host only, not general shell access
